steps: The relationship is linear with the form \(y = mx + b\). For every increase of 1 in \(x\), \(y\) increases by 9, so the slope is \(m = 9\).
Substitute the known point \((x, y) = (2, 23)\) into \(y = 9x + b\): \(23 = 9(2) + b = 18 + b\), so \(b = 5\). The equation is \(y = 9x + 5\).

• A \(y = 2x + 23\): slope is 2, not 9. Eliminated.
  • B \(y = 5x + 9\): when \(x = 2\), \(y = 10 + 9 = 19 \neq 23\). Eliminated.
  • C \(y = 9x + 5\): slope 9 ✓, and at \(x = 2\), \(y = 18 + 5 = 23\) ✓. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D \(y = 3x + 32\): slope is 3, not 9. Eliminated.
